patterns.rule: ALL ViewModels MUST extend BaseMviViewModel — never raw ViewModel()
patterns.good_example: class GoldBuyViewModel @Inject constructor(...) : BaseMviViewModel<State, Event, Command>(GoldBuyFeature) { ... }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #5 #16 #23 #29 #32 #38 #40 — Reviewer B: 'Please use MVI Base View model here. This vm not consistent with architecture'

patterns.rule: Use enums for order statuses — never hardcoded strings like 'COMPLETED', 'ORDER_COMPLETED', 'FAILED'
patterns.good_example: sealed interface OrderStatus { data object Completed : OrderStatus; data object OrderCompleted : OrderStatus }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #17 #18 #22 — Reviewer B: 'Please replace hardcoded strings with enum statuses'

patterns.rule: Dangerous defaults — never hardcode currency 'AED' or country 'AE'. Use required fields or market-aware constants
patterns.good_example: currency = market.currencyCode (required, not nullable)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #6 #25 #44 — Reviewer B: 'These defaults look dangerous, please use required fields. Also currency should not be hardcoded here'

patterns.rule: Screen composables must accept State + accept: (Event) -> Unit — never pass ViewModel directly
patterns.good_example: fun GoldTransactionDetailScreen(state: State, accept: (Event) -> Unit, modifier: Modifier = Modifier)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #35 #39 #45 — Reviewer B: 'Screens should accept State param and have preview. We should not pass view models to screens'

patterns.rule: All screens must have @Preview composable and use AppScreen as root wrapper
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #3 #9 #10 #26 — Reviewer B: 'Can we add preview here?', 'It should use AppScreen and we should have preview here'

patterns.rule: Use SavedStateHandle for fragment arguments — never read arguments manually in Fragment
patterns.good_example: @HiltViewModel class VM @Inject constructor(savedStateHandle: SavedStateHandle) { val orderId = savedStateHandle.get<String>(ARG_ORDER_ID) }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #14 #24 #34 — Reviewer B: 'Please use SavedStateHandle here and inject it to ViewModel constructor'

patterns.rule: Use CoreCommands for toasts — not manual requireContext().showPlusToast()
patterns.good_example: emit Command.CoreCommand.ShowToast(message)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #15 #19 — Reviewer B: 'Toasts we can show with CoreCommands'

patterns.rule: Use constants with named params — never inline magic strings in use case calls
patterns.good_example: goldRepository.onboardUser(userId = userId, countryCode = GoldConstants.UAE.countryCode)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #41 #44 — Reviewer B: 'Please do not use hardcoded - use constants and add comments', 'Please move this to constants and use named params'

patterns.rule: Use sealed class for request modes — replace if-else string construction
patterns.good_example: sealed interface OrderMode { data class Amount(val amount: BigDecimal) : OrderMode; data class Weight(val weight: BigDecimal) : OrderMode }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #42 — Reviewer B: 'Please use sealed class for different modes Request.Amount or Request.Weight'

patterns.rule: Use Theme colors — never Color.White or Color.Black directly
patterns.good_example: color = Theme.colors.fillsSurfaceWhite
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #8 — Reviewer B: 'Can we use colors from Theme?'

patterns.rule: Use Theme typography — never hardcoded fontSize/fontWeight
patterns.good_example: style = Theme.typography.BodyMMedium
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #20 — Reviewer B: 'We should not have hardcoded text styles. All styles should be specified on figma - here we need to use Typography'

patterns.rule: Use base TopBar component — don't create custom Row-based toolbars
patterns.good_example: PlusTopBarWithBack(title = title, onBack = onBack)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #27 — Reviewer B: 'Let's use TopBar component from base components'

patterns.rule: Use PlusButtonLarge/PlusButtonMedium for CTA buttons — never raw Box composable
patterns.good_example: PlusButtonLarge(text = "Continue", state = ButtonState.ENABLED, onClick = onContinue)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #30 #31 — Reviewer B: 'Please use base components for Compose buttons'

patterns.rule: LazyColumn items must have key parameter for performance
patterns.good_example: items(state.frequentlyUsed, key = { it.id }) { BankItem(it) }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #28 — Reviewer B: 'Do we have id here? We need to use ids with Lazy lists for performance'

patterns.rule: OkHttp interceptor for common headers — don't repeat X-User-Id/X-Country on every endpoint
patterns.bad_example: @Header("X-User-Id") userId: String, @Header("X-Country") country: String on every Retrofit method
patterns.good_example: OkHttp Interceptor that adds common headers automatically
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #48 — Reviewer B: 'Can we add these headers on ok http client level once?'

patterns.rule: Handle empty states with a stub/placeholder UI — never silently return
patterns.good_example: if (transactions.isEmpty()) { EmptyStateStub(text = stringResource(R.string.gold_no_transactions)) }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #11 — Reviewer B: 'Please handle empty case with a stub.'

patterns.rule: Don't use nullable when the value is always required in practice
patterns.good_example: beneficiaryAccountId: String (if it's always present in the flow)
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #43 — Reviewer B: 'Can it be null in reality?'

patterns.rule: Document fragile/complex code — especially JS evaluation in WebViews
patterns.bad_example: evaluateJavascript checking for 'payment was successful' text with no comments
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #21 — Reviewer B: 'Please document this decision - this code is unclear and fragile'

patterns.rule: Clean up legacy/backward-compatibility comments — delete dead code instead of marking it
patterns.good_example: Just delete it or refactor it properly
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #12 — Reviewer B: 'Then we can cleanup the code'

patterns.rule: Clean imports — no unused imports, use short imports not fully-qualified inline references
patterns.good_example:
import tech.vance.app.data_layer.network.gold.model.domain.GoldMarket.UAE
... then use UAE directly
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #2 #7 #33 — Reviewer B: 'Please use short imports, it's hard to read these long imports'

patterns.rule: Remove useless comments — code should be self-documenting
patterns.bad_example:
// Group transactions by date
fun groupByDate(...)
patterns.good_example: fun groupByDate(...) // the name is self-explanatory
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #13 — Reviewer B: 'Useless comments - please clean it up'

patterns.rule: Large screens (1000+ lines) should be split into separate composable files by state/section
patterns.good_example: Split: GoldTransactionDetailScreen.kt + GoldTransactionSuccessHeader.kt + GoldTransactionTimeline.kt + GoldTransactionActions.kt
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #37 — Reviewer B: 'This file is too big - please split by states and composable components'

patterns.rule: Add TODO for unimplemented callbacks — don't leave empty lambda bodies
patterns.good_example: onHelpClick = { /* TODO: Wire to help screen */ }
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comment #36 — Reviewer B: 'Should be todo here - otherwise we can forget to implement'

patterns.rule: NEVER modify base components (PlusButton, MainFragment) or build scripts (app.gradle.kts) — keep wealth module isolated
patterns.bad_example: Modifying BasePlusButton from Button to Box implementation
patterns.good_example: If PlusButton doesn't support your use case, create a Gold-specific variant inside ui/gold/components/
patterns.source: PR #XXXX, Comments #1 #46 #47 + Reviewer B Slack: 'I'd prefer to avoid changes to base components (e.g., MainFragment, base button) and not force the resolution strategy in Gradle'

patterns.rule: Sync with develop branch — use existing patterns from recent modules (nre-nro) as reference
patterns.bad_example: Writing new patterns without checking how develop branch handles it
patterns.good_example: git fetch origin develop && git diff origin/develop -- app/src/main/java/tech/vance/app/ui/nre_nro/
patterns.source: PR #XXXX + Reviewer B Slack (2026-03-23): 'Please sync your branches with develop. There are many useful patterns and solid architectural examples (in nre-nro package)'

reviewer_b_high_level_feedback: Sync branches with develop — nre-nro package has gold-standard examples
Follow MVI architecture consistently — complex screens need unit tests
Document complex flows — create docs/ folder inside module with .md files
Use stricter contracts with backend — avoid nullable, default, and hardcoded values
Code outside wealth module should NOT be modified
Split iterations smaller — 16k lines is too hard to review
